news.title: All The Celebrities Spotted At 2026 FIFA World Cup Final Match
news.lede: The 2026 FIFA World Cup Final at MetLife Stadium, pitting Argentina against Spain, transcended a mere sporting event, becoming a colossal entertainment spectacle. The tournament culminated with an unprecedented Halftime Show, curated by Chris Martin, featuring global music titans Madonna, Shakira, Justin Bieber, and BTS, all supporting the FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund. The closing ceremony further amplified the star power with performances from Jennifer Hudson, Post Malone, and Robbie Williams. Off-stage, an extraordinary and diverse roster of celebrities, from Hollywood's Tom Cruise and Matt Damon to sports icons and political figures like Donald Trump, converged, solidifying the final as a monumental cultural fusion.

news.title: 2026 World Cup: All the European sides' fixtures and results
news.lede: Keep track of all the European sides' fixtures and results at the World Cup in Canada, Mexico and the United States. The 2026 FIFA World Cup in Canada, Mexico and the United States kicked off on 11 June and concludes with the final between Spain and holders Argentina in East Rutherford on 19 July. Check out all the details of the European teams' matches. Monday 15 June: D 0-0 vs Cabo Verde (Atlanta) Sunday 21 June: W 4-0 vs Saudi Arabia (Atlanta) Saturday 27 June: W 1-0 vs Uruguay (Guadalajara) Thursday 2 July (R32): W 3-0 vs Austria (Los Angeles) Monday 6 July (R16): W 1-0 vs Portugal (Dallas) Friday 10 July (QF): W 2-1 vs Belgium (Los Angeles) Tuesday 14 July (SF): W 2-0 vs France (Dallas) Sunday 19 July (F): vs Argentina (New York, 21:00).

news.title: A cavalcade of international stars headlined the World Cup's first-ever halftime final show
news.lede: Colombian singer, songwriter and producer Shakira (C), Nigerian singer, songwriter, and record producer Burna Boy, actor Jason Sudeikis gesture with performers at the Half Time Show during the 2026 World Cup football tournament final match between Spain and Argentina at the New York/New Jersey Stadium in East Rutherford on July 19, 2026. Mauro Pimentel/AFP via Getty Images hide caption. As of halftime, the World Cup final Sunday was still anybody's game, with Argentina and Spain tied 0-0. But there's been one winner already – if for nothing but sheer logistics: FIFA managed to pull off its first-ever World Cup final halftime show, featuring a parade of many of the biggest names in music, from Madonna to Coldplay.

news.title: Shakira, Madonna, Justin Bieber and BTS perform at colourful World Cup half-time show
news.lede: Pop giants Madonna, BTS, Shakira and Justin Bieber performed for thousands of spectators at the 2026 World Cup final half-time show. Madonna sung her hit song Music - seeming to appear from tunnels under the arena. Their performances came in a half-time that ran to 27 minutes and 22 seconds - the longest in World Cup history. Colombian pop star Shakira and Nigerian musician Burna Boy reunited on stage after performing together at the tournament's opening ceremony in Mexico City last month.

news.title: Melania Trump Sports American Brands for World Cup Finals Between Spain and Argentina
news.lede: While many of the fans at Sunday’s FIFA World Cup final between Spain and Argentina at MetLife Stadium sported their favorite team’s colors, First Lady Melania Trump is showing off some American spirit. FLOTUS and President Donald Trump aren’t the only dignitaries in the estimated 80,000-person crowd in East Rutherford, N.J.. Spain’s Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez, Mexico’s President Claudia Sheinbaum and Canada’s Prime Minister Mark Carney were expected, as well as Spain’s King Felipe VI and Queen Letizia, who flew in with their daughters, Princess Leonor and Infanta Sofía. The Trumps were seated with FIFA president Gianni Infantino and his wife Leena Al Ashqar.

news.title: BTS, Madonna and the Muppets light up Fifa World Cup half-time show
news.lede: Musical headliners Madonna, Shakira, Justin Bieber, BTS and Coldplay were joined by Brazilian football icons and Ted Lasso actors in a spectacular first-ever Fifa World Cup final halftime show on Sunday. The star-packed, Super Bowl-style extravaganza – a first for football’s showpiece event – also featured a tribute to football icon Pele, and a rendition of “Seven Nation Army” led by Gustavo Dudamel and featuring musicians from the US, Venezuela and Iran. Sesame Street and The Muppets characters joined Coldplay and a New York school choir to close a performance designed to raise money for the Fifa Global Citizen Education Fund, which promotes access to sport for underprivileged children. The 12-minute performance meant the total halftime break in the match between Spain and Argentina lasted 27 minutes.

news.title: FIFA's first World Cup halftime show was a game changer. But was it good?
news.lede: For the first time in FIFA World Cup history, futbol took a cue from football and sandwiched a star-studded show between its two halves of play. Madonna, Shakira, Justin Bieber and BTS represented the cultural diversity of the World Cup, which began June 11 and culminated with its 104th match at the July 19 final between defending champion Argentina and Spain. The 11-minute halftime show at New York New Jersey Stadium (aka MetLife Stadium) was an exercise in collaboration. Performing atop a rainbow-colored banner covering the field, echoes of Madonna’s “Music” filled the screen before unveiling the Queen of Pop in her now-signature pink as she rode in a car packed with vivacious dancers hanging off the sides in a pre-taped intro.

news.title: Four Observations from the World Cup third place match
news.lede: The third place match of this World Cup was something else. Four goals from England in the first half made it seem that we were not going to see a close game, but France had something to say about it. We saw records matched and overcome, and two key goals in the Golden Boot race. This was the highest scoring match in this edition of the tournament — surpassing Germany’s 7-1 win over Curacao.
